question: I need a LAMMPS interatomic potential suitable for pure aluminum in the liquid-to–warm dense regime to compute ionic transport properties via classical MD (no DFT-MD). Target conditions: T ≈ 1,000–60,000 K; densities near isochoric ρ ≈ 2.70 g/cm³ (and, if available, 1–3 g/cm³). Properties of interest: self-diffusion, shear viscosity, and the ionic contribution to thermal conductivity (via Green–Kubo or Müller–Plathe). Please evaluate and compare the applicability and validation of: (a) EAM/MEAM (e.g., Mishin) parameterizations; (b) charge-transfer potentials like COMB3/ReaxFF; (c) screened Coulomb models (Yukawa) with short-range ZBL; and (d) temperature- and density-dependent effective ion potentials derived from average-atom/pseudoatom methods (e.g., PAMD, Purgatorio) or OF-DFT. For your recommended choice, justify its validity across partial ionization up to ~60 kK and its ability to reproduce structure and transport (with references/benchmarks vs DFT-MD or experiment for Al), and provide LAMMPS specifics: pair_style(s) to use (e.g., table, yukawa, zbl, hybrid/overlay), how to obtain Z*(T,ρ) and screening lengths, how to splice ZBL at short range, suggested cutoffs/timesteps, and any caveats for computing heat flux with many-body potentials. Include example input snippets and key citations.

Identify the best interatomic potential for MD simulations of aluminum crystallization from an undercooled melt (about 0.6–1.0 Tm). Prioritize potentials that accurately reproduce melting-related properties (Tm ≈ 933 K within ~±50 K, latent heat of fusion, volume change on melting, solid–liquid coexistence densities, solid–liquid interfacial free energy, and crystal growth velocity vs undercooling) and realistic liquid structure/transport near Tm (RDF/structure factor matching X-ray/neutron data such as Waseda, self-diffusion, viscosity). Compare leading EAM/MEAM options (e.g., Mishin 1999/2004/2013 Al, Zhou 2004, Grochola 2005, Liu 2004) with recent machine-learned Al potentials (SNAP, GAP, MTP/NNP) that include liquid/coexistence training. Cite benchmarks/literature for melting and liquid properties, note known limitations for nucleation/growth kinetics, and recommend a specific, publicly available potential (with exact LAMMPS file/name) best validated for crystallization from undercooled Al.

Identify the specific classical MD interatomic potential for aluminum that was explicitly parameterized using a finite-temperature thermodynamic fitting approach (free-energy/thermodynamic property matching) to reproduce room-temperature thermoelastic behavior—i.e., accurate second- and third-order elastic constants (SOEC and TOEC), along with related properties such as thermal expansion and heat capacity—at T ≥ 300 K. Provide the potential name and type (EAM/MEAM or similar), the original publication and DOI, and links to parameter files usable in LAMMPS. Include keywords: aluminum Al, thermoelastic constants, third-order elastic constants, anharmonic elasticity, temperature-dependent fitting, quasi-harmonic/Grüneisen, thermodynamic inversion.

Identify a classical interatomic potential for fcc aluminum that corrects the Ercolessi–Adams (1994) glue model’s low relaxed intrinsic stacking fault energy while preserving comparable surface energies and thermophysical properties (melting point, thermal expansion, elastic constants). Specify the recommended potential (name, authors, year), its reported relaxed gamma_ISF value (target ≈135–170 mJ/m²), evidence for accurate surface/thermal properties, and provide a citation and common LAMMPS potential file name if available.

In molecular dynamics/TTM-MD simulations of femtosecond laser–induced ablation and spallation in aluminum, which specific EAM potential(s) best reproduce both (a) the Al shock Hugoniot (P–us–up and EOS up to ~100–200 GPa, reasonable Grüneisen parameter and release isentrope) and (b) realistic melting behavior (Tm ≈ 933 K, latent heat, liquid density, and pressure-dependent melting slope)? Please compare commonly used options (e.g., Mishin 1999/2004, Zhou–Johnson–Wadley 2004, Ercolessi–Adams, Mendelev, Winey–Gupta), summarize validation vs experiment/DFT, note trade-offs affecting spall strength predictions, and provide citations and the corresponding LAMMPS potential file names.

Identify the specific Physically Informed Neural Network (PINN) interatomic potential for elemental aluminum that is validated as a general-purpose, transferable model across solids and liquids. Requirements:
- Accurately reproduces point/line/planar defect properties (vacancies, interstitials, dislocations, stacking faults), melting behavior, liquid thermodynamics/transport (RDF, diffusion, viscosity), surface energies and liquid surface tension, and crack initiation/propagation.
- Computationally efficient for large-scale LAMMPS MD.

Please provide:
- Exact model name/version and authors (e.g., Mishin group PINN-Al, year), with DOI and a link to downloadable LAMMPS-compatible files.
- Brief description of the DFT training set (phases/structures covered, volumes/strains, surfaces, liquids, defects).
- Published validation/benchmarks vs DFT and experiment (elastic constants, vacancy/SF energies, melting point, liquid properties, surface tension, fracture/crack growth), and comparisons to EAM/MEAM, GAP, MTP, SNAP, BP-NNP.
- Known limitations or failure modes, and any guidance on time step/cutoff and recommended temperature/pressure ranges.

For aluminum (fcc Al) simulations where I must compute thermodynamic quantities that require reliable per-atom energies (e.g., surface melting temperatures, free energies) in addition to mechanical response (stress–strain), which machine-learning interatomic potential should be avoided because it is force-only (predicts forces without an explicit energy model and does not output atomic energies)?

Identify Al–Cu interatomic potentials with explicit Al–Cu cross-interaction parameters (for LAMMPS) that have been validated to reproduce the θ (Al2Cu, tetragonal I4/mcm) and θ′ (Al2Cu, tetragonal I4/mmm) precipitates’ lattice parameters, 0 K formation enthalpies, and their correct relative stability when temperature effects are included (up to ~600°C). Compare EAM vs 2NN-MEAM vs newer MLIPs, citing specific parameterizations (e.g., Zhou–Johnson 2004 EAM alloy, Kim/Baskes 2NN‑MEAM Al–Cu, Mendelev/Mishin variants, OpenKIM/NIST models), report quantitative errors versus DFT/experiment for θ/θ′, note temperature range of reliability and known limitations, and provide LAMMPS file names/DOIs or OpenKIM IDs.

Rewritten query: Identify an interatomic potential for classical MD that can explicitly handle bond breaking/formation and charge transfer to model hydrogen in Al–Cu alloys, including: (i) H2 dissociative adsorption and chemisorption on Al(111), Cu(111), and Al–Cu alloy surfaces; (ii) H diffusion and trapping in bulk Al/Cu and at Al–Cu intermetallics (e.g., Al2Cu/θ′ precipitates); and (iii) Al–Cu–H thermodynamics and phase stability (hydride formation, solubility) over 300–800 K and ~1–100 bar. Prefer LAMMPS-compatible reactive potentials with validated Al–Cu–H cross-interactions (e.g., ReaxFF or COMB3); alternatively, MEAM/2NN‑MEAM or MLIPs trained to DFT that reproduce H2 dissociation barriers and adsorption energies. Which published parameter sets meet these requirements and are available for download?

Which Ni–Al interatomic potential (Embedded-Atom Method) was explicitly developed for Ni-based superalloys to model γ-Ni (fcc Ni with Al) and γ′-Ni3Al (L12), reproducing correct thermodynamics and melting temperatures and validated for dislocation migration, elastic constants, and stacking-fault energies? Please identify the specific potential (authors/year, e.g., Purja Pun & Mishin 2012 EAM) and its citation or LAMMPS file name.

Identify Ni–Al–Cr interatomic potentials (EAM/MEAM/ADP) validated for γ/γ′ Ni-based superalloys that accurately reproduce planar-fault energetics and Cr segregation effects in γ′-Ni3Al (L12), including APB(111)/(100), SISF/SESF/CSF, and {111} twin boundary energies. Specifically seek potentials shown to capture Cr segregation to superlattice stacking faults and its impact on microtwin nucleation/growth. Provide citations and accessible LAMMPS/OpenKIM potential files. Keywords: “Ni3Al γ′”, “superlattice stacking fault (SISF/SESF)”, “APB energy”, “twin boundary”, “chromium segregation”, “microtwin”, “EAM”, “MEAM”, “Ni–Al–Cr”.

Identify interatomic potentials for LAMMPS (EAM/MEAM/ADP) that accurately model the B2 (CsCl) → L1_0 (CuAu-type) martensitic transformation in Ni–Al–Co. Requirements:
- Ternary parameterization with consistent Ni–Al, Ni–Co, and Al–Co cross-interactions (single ternary potential preferred; otherwise a validated cross-compatible binary set).
- Reproduces energy ordering and Bain path between B2 and L1_0, L1_0 tetragonality (c/a), elastic constants, phonon stability, and stacking-fault/antiphase-boundary energies.
- Predicts reasonable composition/temperature dependence of phase stability and transformation (e.g., 45–55 at.% Al with 0–20 at.% Co, 300–1200 K), consistent with DFT/experimental data (phase diagram, ordering temperatures, Ms).
Please provide specific potential names/files (e.g., .eam.alloy or 2NN‑MEAM parameter sets), LAMMPS pair_style compatibility, and citation/DOI/download links (e.g., Mishin-type Ni–Al EAM with Co extensions, 2NN‑MEAM sets including Ni/Al/Co).

Identify a single, transferable second-nearest-neighbor MEAM (2NN-MEAM) parameter library that provides consistent cross-interactions for Cu, Ti, Ni, Cr, Co, Al, Fe, and Mn, suitable for simulating dislocation cores, stacking-fault energies, and mechanical behavior in complex concentrated/high-entropy alloys (e.g., CoCrFeMnNi) using LAMMPS/OpenKIM. Accurate temperature-dependent calibration is not required. Which MEAM potential suite/database fits these criteria?

Identify a published, LAMMPS-compatible angular-dependent Al–H interatomic potential (e.g., 2NN-MEAM/MEAM or ADP, possibly ReaxFF) that has been validated for dilute H in fcc Al—specifically reproducing (i) solution energies and correct site preference (tetrahedral lower than octahedral), (ii) H migration barrier along the T–O–T path ≈0.15–0.25 eV, and (iii) bulk Al lattice/elastic properties; cite the exact parameter set (authors/year/DOI) and where to obtain the files.

Identify a published, open-source machine-learning interatomic potential for the Ti–Al–V system (covering pure Ti, Ti–Al, Ti–V; Ti‑6Al‑4V/Ti‑64) that achieves near-DFT accuracy and has been validated to: (1) reproduce the α↔β (hcp↔bcc) transition in Ti, (2) capture α↔D019 (Ti3Al) ordering in Ti–Al, and (3) represent β+ω coexistence in Ti–V (ω phase stability under alloying/pressure). Prefer potentials usable in LAMMPS/ASE (e.g., GAP, ACE/PACE, MTP, DeepMD/DP‑GEN, SNAP) with available parameter files, citations/DOIs, and validation showing accurate energies, elastic constants, phonons, defect/stacking-fault energies, and reported transition temperatures/phase stabilities for microstructure-scale MD of Ti‑6Al‑4V.
